content:
Case 20:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 77
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Progressive dyspnea, ascites, and a painful gluteal mass in a patient with CLL
- Vital signs: BP 105/65 mmHg, HR 112/min, RR 22/min, Temp 37.7 °C, SpO₂ 92%
Setting: Acute hematology intake
Capabilities: Rapid bedside assessment with point-of-care imaging and routine laboratory panels is available, but invasive sampling or specialized molecular diagnostics are not yet ready.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-09-23: Presents with progressive dyspnea, abdominal distension, and painful swelling of the left gluteal region with induration over old scars from animal bites. Left leg edema present. Pulmonary exam: diminished breath sounds with basal crackles. Vital signs as listed. Current meds: beclometasone/formoterol inhaler, HCTZ, levothyroxine, vitamins, pantoprazole, amlodipine, aciclovir, allopurinol, bisoprolol, candesartan, atorvastatin, zanubrutinib 160 mg daily.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Baseline Labs:
Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC 41 ×10⁹/L (82% lymphocytes), Plt 78 ×10⁹/L. Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.1, fibrinogen 281 mg/dL, D-dimer 2987 µg/L FEU. Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.4 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, AST 185 U/L, ALT 210 U/L, bilirubin 2.8 mg/dL, LDH 852 U/L, uric acid 7.8 mg/dL, albumin 2.8 g/dL, total protein 5.2 g/dL, NT-proBNP 780 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.02 ng/mL, IgG 359 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "point-of-care ultrasound of abdomen and gluteal mass"}}

response.request: point-of-care ultrasound of abdomen and gluteal mass

content:
Initial Imaging:
Chest X-ray: bilateral pleural effusions with basal atelectasis (right > left); Abdominal ultrasound: four-quadrant ascites, multiple enlarged paraaortic and mesenteric nodes up to 6.5 cm, bulky hepatic hilum conglomerate, hepatomegaly with nodular contour, splenomegaly 18.2 × 9.6 cm. Echocardiogram: LVEF 51% with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bedside ultrasound of the gluteal mass"}}

response.request: bedside ultrasound of the gluteal mass

content: 'initial_imaging' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "ascitic fluid analysis"}}

'ascitic fluid analysis' not available. Try 'initial_imaging' instead.
This case does not include a dedicated ascitic/paracentesis fluid analysis. You could request abdominal ultrasound or other imaging findings (initial_imaging) to evaluate for ascites, or baseline labs if you want fluid-related lab context.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "peripheral blood smear"}}

'peripheral blood smear' not available. Try 'baseline_labs' instead.
This case doesn't have a dedicated peripheral blood smear item. You can request baseline labs (CBC) which provides peripheral blood cell counts and is available in this round.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Large cell transformation (Richter syndrome) in a patient with CLL, with likely lymphomatous infiltration of intra-abdominal organs and gluteal mass, and possible superimposed infection.", "differentials": ["Progression of chronic lymphocytic leukemia with organ infiltration", "Infectious complications (e.g. bacterial soft tissue infection, abscess or cellulitis)", "Venous thromboembolism with secondary lymphedema"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Start broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotics", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Arrange urgent tissue biopsy of gluteal mass (when feasible)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Begin supportive care with intravenous fluids, supplemental oxygen, and monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consult hematology-oncology for consideration of transformation and tailored cytotoxic therapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Large cell transformation (Richter syndrome) in a patient with CLL, with likely lymphomatous infiltration of intra-abdominal organs and gluteal mass, and possible superimposed infection.
differentials: Progression of chronic lymphocytic leukemia with organ infiltration
Infectious complications (e.g. bacterial soft tissue infection, abscess or cellulitis)

treatment_plan.action: Arrange urgent tissue biopsy of gluteal mass (when feasible)
Begin supportive care with intravenous fluids, supplemental oxygen, and monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome
Consult hematology-oncology for consideration of transformation and tailored cytotoxic therapy

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient diagnostic unit
Capabilities: The patient can undergo targeted imaging, bone marrow or tissue sampling, and comprehensive flow cytometry while longer-turnaround molecular studies are still pending.
Request additional information or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "core needle biopsy of the gluteal mass"}}

response.request: core needle biopsy of the gluteal mass

content:
Extended Evaluations:
CT thorax (2025-09-26): segmental pulmonary embolism right lower lobe with infarction/infarct pneumonia; bilateral pleural effusions. PET-CT (2025-09-30): intensely FDG-avid nodal disease (cervical to pelvic), bulky retroperitoneal/hepatic nodes, avid gluteal mass, uptake in infarct zone. Gluteal biopsy (10-01): diffuse large B-cell lymphoma CD19⁺/CD20⁺/PAX5⁺/CD5⁺/CD23⁻, Ki-67 ~80%. Lumbar puncture: clear CSF, no malignant cells.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter syndrome (transformation of CLL to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma) with high tumor burden, presenting with bulky nodal and extranodal disease and a gluteal soft-tissue mass, complicated by pulmonary embolism and infarct pneumonia.", "differentials": ["CLL progression without transformation", "Infectious soft tissue process", "Other high-grade lymphoma (e.g. de novo DLBCL unrelated to CLL)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate urgent anthracycline-based chemotherapy for DLBCL (e.g., R-CHOP or R-CHOEP as appropriate given comorbidities)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Anticoagulation for pulmonary embolism if clinically safe", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate supportive care: antimicrobial prophylaxis, tumor lysis syndrome prevention with hydration and allopurinol/rasburicase, transfusion support as needed", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Arrange multidisciplinary management and early palliative/supportive care involvement given aggressive disease and comorbidities", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter syndrome (transformation of CLL to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma) with high tumor burden, presenting with bulky nodal and extranodal disease and a gluteal soft-tissue mass, complicated by pulmonary embolism and infarct pneumonia.
differentials: Other high-grade lymphoma (e.g. de novo DLBCL unrelated to CLL)

treatment_plan.action: Initiate urgent anthracycline-based chemotherapy for DLBCL (e.g., R-CHOP or R-CHOEP as appropriate given comorbidities)
Anticoagulation for pulmonary embolism if clinically safe
Initiate supportive care: antimicrobial prophylaxis, tumor lysis syndrome prevention with hydration and allopurinol/rasburicase, transfusion support as needed
Arrange multidisciplinary management and early palliative/supportive care involvement given aggressive disease and comorbidities

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Definitive staging and planning phase
Capabilities: Full cytogenetic, molecular, and response assessments plus historical treatment data are accessible to guide transplant or cellular therapy planning.
Request additional information or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetic and molecular analysis of the gluteal mass biopsy and CLL cells"}}

response.request: cytogenetic and molecular analysis of the gluteal mass biopsy and CLL cells

content:
Molecular Profile:
Sequencing: TP53 mutation VAF 68%, NOTCH1 frameshift VAF 42%, SF3B1 VAF 38%, BTK C481S resistance mutation; IgHV unmutated, ZAP-70 positive.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter transformation of CLL (to CD5+, CD23\u2013 diffuse large B-cell lymphoma) with high-risk molecular features (TP53, NOTCH1, SF3B1 mutations, BTK resistance) and disseminated, aggressive disease.", "differentials": ["Double/triple-hit DLBCL (MYC/BCL2/BCL6 rearranged)", "De novo DLBCL in setting of pre-existing CLL", "Refractory CLL with high-grade transformation"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate intensive chemoimmunotherapy for Richter syndrome, considering modified R-EPOCH or R-CHOP but anticipate refractoriness due to TP53 mutation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Evaluate for enrolment in clinical trials for novel agents (e.g., BCL2 inhibitors, bispecific antibodies, non-covalent BTK inhibitors) due to BTK resistance and poor-risk genetics", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Early allogeneic stem cell transplantation workup (if performance status allows)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Continue best supportive care including VTE anticoagulation, anti-infective prophylaxis, and tumor lysis syndrome prevention", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter transformation of CLL (to CD5+, CD23– diffuse large B-cell lymphoma) with high-risk molecular features (TP53, NOTCH1, SF3B1 mutations, BTK resistance) and disseminated, aggressive disease.
differentials: De novo DLBCL in setting of pre-existing CLL

treatment_plan.action: Initiate intensive chemoimmunotherapy for Richter syndrome, considering modified R-EPOCH or R-CHOP but anticipate refractoriness due to TP53 mutation
Evaluate for enrolment in clinical trials for novel agents (e.g., BCL2 inhibitors, bispecific antibodies, non-covalent BTK inhibitors) due to BTK resistance and poor-risk genetics
Early allogeneic stem cell transplantation workup (if performance status allows)
Continue best supportive care including VTE anticoagulation, anti-infective prophylaxis, and tumor lysis syndrome prevention
